title: For rare mitochondrial disease, a small molecule shows promise
summary: In a Phase 2b trial, Khondrion’s sonlicromanol led to significant health benefits for patients with mitochondrial disease linked to a common mutation.

title: Can nature-based therapies heal people and the planet? Study investigates holistic solutions
summary: Researchers at Swansea University have developed a protocol for a scoping review to explore how nature-based interventions influence individual, collective, and planetary well-being.

title: IL-12 cytokine factory demonstrates success in targeting hard-to-treat cancers
summary: A team of researchers from the Rice Biotech Launch Pad at Rice University has developed an implantable "cytokine factory" that safely triggers potent immune responses against hard-to-treat cancers, including metastatic melanoma, pancreatic and colorectal tumors.

title: Study highlights pulmonary artery denervation as a key intervention for pulmonary hypertension
summary: A recent publication in the International Journal of Cardiology, Pulmonary artery denervation in pulmonary hypertension: A comprehensive meta-analysis, has shed light on the potential of pulmonary artery denervation (PADN) as an innovative intervention for pulmonary hypertension (PH), a condition that places patients at risk for right heart failure and death.

title: Food-as-medicine could save billions in healthcare costs-here’s what’s standing in its way
summary: Researchers review how Food-as-Medicine (FAM) interventions can improve health, reduce healthcare costs, and reshape food production systems. They emphasize the urgent need for clinical validation before scaling individualized nutrition solutions.

title: Queensland melioidosis death toll climbs after floods spread soil-borne bacteria
summary: Melioidosis claims the life of another Queensland Health patient in the Townsville region as a microbiologist warns floodwater is pushing dangerous bacteria to the soil's surface.

title: New discovery promises to revolutionize liver organoid development
summary: Organoids are small-scale models that mimic human organs. These models could help scientists understand diseases and test treatments, and potentially aid in regenerative therapeutic methods.

title: Statins Could Reduce Dementia Risk Even in People With Low Cholesterol
summary: A new study has found a link between 'bad' cholesterol and the risk of dementia.

title: Anxiety fuels emotional eating and weight gain, study finds
summary:
Emotional eating and body dissatisfaction mediate links between BMI changes and mental health.
These psychosocial factors explain how anxiety, depression, and weight gain interrelate over time.

title: ‘My blood sugar went up despite diet and exercise. Was I drinking coconut water and watermelon juice all wrong?’
summary: Here’s a guide on how to have home-blended fruit juices safely
